diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic sign of opioid-induced respiratory depression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Empty pill bottles and known opioid use disorder
Availability of a take-home naloxone kit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms an available bridging treatment while awaiting paramedics.

symptoms.symptom_name: GCS 3, bilateral pinpoint pupils, RR 6, SpO2 82%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unresponsiveness with severe respiratory depression, cyanosis, and known opioid use/empty pill bottles together confirm a life-threatening opioid overdose.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate naloxone administration (if available) and airway/breathing support
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reverses opioid-induced respiratory depression; the only rapid life-saving bystander action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency transport for continued monitoring, repeat naloxone dosing as needed, and toxicology screening
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required given the risk of re-sedation and potential poly-substance ingestion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Waiting to see if he wakes up on his own without giving naloxone or calling for help
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would risk fatal respiratory arrest within minutes.
